Patch Set 4:

Looks good to me.
Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 4:

This would be better (and maybe clearer) if the option could be given in the form ping=(yes)|(no)|(auto) with auto as the default.

Patch Set 4:

Hm...
Does it ever make sense on the client to ever have ping=no? Probably not?

Patch Set 4:

It wouldn't make sense to specify ping=no since this option is specifically for systems that want to overwrite the "suppress_pings" and ping=no would make it redundant and confusing. I would elect to keep the current implementation unless there is strong objection.

Patch Set 4:

It might make sense to allow a "ping=no" mount option so that pings can be suppressed on specific clients, rather than setting the global "suppress_pings" option.

Patch Set 4:

If I understand correctly that server would assume client ping if there is no OBD_CONNECT_PINGLESS support for backward compatibility. That is where we take advantage of it with the always_ping implementation that we just negate the flag before connecting to the server.  

If we implement ping=no, we would need another connection flag to tell the server not to check ping and evict client regardless suppress_pings or not. Is this what we want? If so the options will be:

ping=yes (same as always-ping implemented in the patch so far)
ping=auto (client ping unless server suppress_pings, the existing code)
ping=no (client does not ping, regardless server suppress_pings or not)

Patch Set 4:

IIRC, we wanted clients to _propose_ whether they are able to or would like to ping, while servers _decide_ whether a client has to ping or not.
Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 4:

Could we make a decision whether to go forward to implement the
additional ping=no? For our original purposes the current
implementation of always_ping option is sufficient. However if we
implement ping=no, it may deviate from the original design that Li
mentioned and client could 'force' the decision to not ping. 
And I think we will need an additional connection flag to achieve that.
Patch Set 4: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 6707ed7038dd6208cc6e1e56d88fba8eec5e92cb

parents.subject: LU-5505 clio: revise read ahead algorithm

commit.subject: LU-6391 llite: Add client mount opt to ignore suppress_pings
commit.message:
LU-6391 llite: Add client mount opt to ignore suppress_pings

When Lustre servers enable 'suppress_pings', all clients will stop
pinging. However, some clients may not have external mechanism
to notify Lustre servers for node death and therefore need to
preserve the Lustre ping.

This patch provides a mount option 'always_ping' so that the
client will not stop pinging even if the server has enabled
'suppress_pings'.

Signed-off-by: Wally Wang <wang@cray.com>
Change-Id: Ia7b45e8d2dbb53f02157ef2ab1d327d9483c2455
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/14127
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Li Wei <wei.g.li@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Chris Horn <hornc@cray.com>
Reviewed-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
